pi_items.source: Wikipedia (https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Roman_numerals), Britannica, UNRV, and Cuemath all confirm I = 1
Wikipedia (https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Roman_numerals), Britannica, UNRV, and Cuemath all confirm V = 5
Wikipedia (https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Roman_numerals), Britannica, UNRV, and Cuemath all confirm X = 10
Wikipedia (https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Roman_numerals), Britannica, UNRV, and Cuemath all confirm L = 50
Wikipedia (https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Roman_numerals), Britannica, UNRV, and Cuemath all confirm C = 100
Wikipedia (https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Roman_numerals), Britannica, UNRV, and Cuemath all confirm D = 500
Wikipedia (https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Roman_numerals), Britannica, UNRV, and Cuemath all confirm M = 1000

privileged_info_summary: The Roman numeral reference values specified in task metadata are correct. The seven standard Roman numeral symbols and their corresponding values are verified: I (1), V (5), X (10), L (50), C (100), D (500), M (1000). These values are consistent across multiple authoritative sources including Wikipedia, Britannica, UNRV, and Cuemath.
